mechanism_narrative: An increase in CPIAUCSL over the prior month signals accelerating inflation that compresses real corporate margins and triggers tighter monetary policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns follow rising CPIAUCSL in more than 50% of monthly observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate above 5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs for non-financial corporates, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and falling retail sales signal a self-reinforcing contraction in household income and consumer expenditures that amplifies recession probability, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of UNRATE and RSAFS produces a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts during a restrictive monetary policy environment foreshadow a contraction in interest-sensitive durable spending and construction employment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with FEDFUNDS above 4%
failure_condition: HOUST declines coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of weeks when FEDFUNDS is above 4% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and falling capacity utilization that reduces corporate pricing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in over 55% of instances during walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in retail sales reflects strengthening consumer demand that supports top-line revenue growth for consumer-facing corporates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a tightening labor market that boosts aggregate wage income and household consumption forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 55% of monthly observations during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4% indicates labor market slack that dampens wage growth expectations and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in over 50% of episodes when UNRATE exceeds 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window.
